extra.message: User-controlled input 'name' from POST /students/ is interpolated directly into SQL query using string formatting (% operator). An attacker can inject malicious SQL via the student name field, enabling data exfiltration, modification, or deletion.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: Jinja2 is configured with autoescape=False, disabling automatic HTML escaping. User-controlled data rendered in templates (e.g., student names, course titles, review text in course.jinja2:14,15,22 and students.jinja2:16) will be rendered raw, enabling stored and reflected XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: CSRF middleware is commented out, leaving all POST endpoints vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery. An attacker can trick authenticated users into performing unauthorized actions (creating students, courses, reviews, marks).

Password verification uses MD5 hashing, which is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage. MD5 is vulnerable to collision attacks and can be brute-forced rapidly, enabling credential compromise if password hashes are leaked.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: Session cookie is configured with httponly=False, allowing JavaScript access to the session cookie. Combined with XSS vulnerabilities, this enables session hijacking attacks.

POST handler for /students/ creates new students without any authentication or authorization check. The @authorize() decorator is only used on logout(). Unauthenticated users can create arbitrary student records.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: POST handler for /courses/ creates new courses without any authentication or authorization check. Unauthenticated users can create arbitrary course records.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: POST handler for /courses/{course_id}/review creates reviews without any authentication check. Unauthenticated users can create arbitrary reviews.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: POST handler for /students/{student_id}/evaluate/{course_id} creates marks/grades without any authentication check. Unauthenticated users can assign arbitrary grades to students.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Application is configured with debug=True in production code. This can expose sensitive information including stack traces, environment variables, and configuration details in error responses.

cwe: CWE-215: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code
CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
